purpose: F4 leg-class friction multiplier: calibration dataset, OLS fits, and friction_closures.py implementation
fit_model: f_corrected / f_lam = a + b / Re  (OLS, linear in 1/Re)
limitations: Only 3 data points per class (heater, cooler, downcomer); n=9 for upcomer
Ri correction NOT included — wall-bulk deltaT per leg not yet extracted
Upcomer R2=0.015 (essentially no predictive value as a single class)
Coarse mesh only — no GCI bounds (T6 blocked)
Valid Re range approx 60-135 (Salt 2-4 operating band only)
Driver-side thermal mismatch (~60 K) limits mdot improvement from friction alone
Corrected Salt Q perturbations excluded pending formal requalification gate
buoyancy_note: F4 is a SHEAR/RESISTANCE correction only. buoyancy_pressure() in solver.py handles the hydrostatic term separately. The de-buoyed f_corrected_over_flam from momentum_budget.json was used as the fit target.
next_steps: Extract wall-bulk deltaT per leg from sample_segment_htc_uaprime.py outputs
Compute section-median Ri per leg (use MEDIAN not mean; see CLAUDE.md gotcha #4)
Fit Ri-corrected form: f/f_lam = f3h_ratio * (1 + C_leg * max(Ri_streamwise, 0)^n)
Wire F4_leg_class into solver.py ScenarioConfig via friction_form='F4_leg_class'
Add corrected Salt Q rows once formal gate issues requalified verdict
